text: KILMOVEE, a parish 4 m. W by S of Ballaghadireen, co. Mayo. Area 20,756 acres. Pop. 5,844.
KILMOYLAN, a parish 5 m. S by E of Tuam, co. Galway. Area 8,567 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,788; in 1841, 1,903.—Also a parish in co. Limerick. Area 15,091 acres. Pop. in 1841, 3,350.
KILMUCKRIDGE, a parish 4 m. ENE of Oulart, co. Wexford, Leinster. Area 3,898 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,380; in 1841, 1,602.
KILMUD, a parish 24 m. NNW of Killinchy, co. Down. Area 4,634 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,154.

KILMUIR, a parish of Inverness-shire, forming the N extremity of the isle of Skye. It is 16 m. in length, and 8 m. in breadth. The inhabited part, along the coast, is flat with gently rising eminences; but the interior is mountainous, and covered with heath. The extent of sea-coast is upwards of 30 m. Pop. in 1801, 2,555; in 1831, 3,415; in 1841, 3,625.

KILMUIR EASTER, a parish situated partly in the co. of Ross, and partly in that of Cromarty, on the frith of Cromarty. There are three villages in the p., viz., Milntown, Bartaraville, and Portlich. Of these Milntown, the principal, has a pop. of about 200. Pop. of p. in 1831, 1,551; in 1841, 1,486.
KILMUN, a pleasing little village at the head of the Holy loch, in the district of Cowal, Argyleshire.

KILMURRY, a parish 6 m. SE of Macroom, co. Cork. Area 9,109 acres. Pop. in 1841, 3,742.— Also a parish 21 m. ESE of Limerick, co. Limerick. Area 3,570 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,803; in 1841, 1,708.—Also a parish 3 m. WNW of Carrick-on- Suir, co. Tipperary. Area 7,275 acres. Pop. 2,478. KIL
KILMURRY-CLONDERALAW, a parish in co. Clare. Area 10,457 acres. Pop. in 1841, 4,332. The surface extends along the W of Clonderalaw bay.
KILMURRY-ELY, a parish 13 m. NNW of Shinrone, King's co. Area 5,385 acres. Pop. 1,655.

KILMURRY-IBRICKANE, a parish 23 m. W by S of Milltown-Malbay, co. Clare. Area 25,887 acres, of which 331 acres are in Lough Doo. Pop. in 1831, 8,433; in 1841, 10,747. Slieve - Callan on the E boundary has an alt. of 1,282 ft.

KILMURRY-NEGAUL, a parish and village in co. Clare. Area of p. 2,917 acres. Pop. in 1831, 628; in 1841, 699; of whom 137 were in the v., which is <math>2\frac{1}{3}</math> m. N by W of Six-Mile-Bridge.
KILNABRONOGUE, a parish 25 m. N of Eyrecourt, co. Galway. Area 3,552 acres. Pop. 1,126.
KILNAGARIFF, a parish 2 m. SE of Castle- Connell, co. Limerick. Area 4,455 acres. Pop. 2,724.
KILNAGLORY, a parish 43 m. WSW of Cork, in co. Cork. Area 3,942 acres. Pop. 1,080.
KILNAGROSS, a parish 13 m. NNE of Clonakilty, co. Cork. Area 3,764 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,060.
KILNALECK, a village in the p. of Kildrumferton, co. Cavan, 34 m. NW of Mount-Nugent. Pop. in 1841, 327.

KILNAMANAGH, a parish 21 m. NE of Frenchpark, co. Roscommon. Area 7,621 acres, of which 746 acres are in Lough Gara. Pop. in 1831, 2,815; in 1841, 2,900.—Also a cluster of bogs in co. Roscommon, lying chiefly S and E of Breeogue bridge. Area 3,792 acres.—Also a parish 9 m. SW of Castletown-Berehaven, co. Cork. Area 13,810 acres. Pop. in 1831, 5,612; in 1841, 5,861.—Also a parish 14 m. NE of Oulart, co. Wexford. Area 2,678 acres. Pop. in 1831, 759; in 1841, 870.
KILNAMARTRY, a parish 3½ m. SW by W of Macroom, co. Cork. Area 11,680 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,604; in 1841, 2,782.
KILNANEAVE, or KILLANEAFE, a parish 44 m. SE by S of Nenagh, co. Tipperary. Area 6,607 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,136; in 1841, 2,021.
KILNASOOLAGH, a parish 3 m. W of Newmarket-on-Fergus, co. Clare. Area 5,138 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,319; in 1841, 1,158. The surface forms the E screen of the upper part of the Fergus estuary.
KILNAUGHTEN, a parish in co. Kerry, containing the town of Tarbert. Area 9,164 acres. Pop. in 1831, 4,371; in 1841, 5,102.
KILNEBOY, a parish in co. Clare. Area 17,967 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,678; in 1841, 4,102.
KILNEHUE, or Lamogue, a parish 35 m. NW by W of Gorey, co. Wexford. Area 15,360 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,393; in 1841, 3,596.
KILNEMONA, a parish 33 m. WNW of Ennis, co. Clare. Area 5,418 acres. Pop. in 1841, 1,758.
KILNERATH, a parish 2 m. NW by N of Newport-Tip., co. Tipperary. Area 10,449 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,749; in 1841, 2,970.
KILNINIAN AND KILMORE, a parish in Argyleshire, forming a peninsula in the NW extremity of the island of Mull, extending 12 m. in length, and nearly of the same breadth. Pop. in 1841, 4,335.
KILNINVER, a parish in Lorn, in Argyleshire, united with the parish of Kilmelfort, and forming together nearly a square of 12 m. bounded on the W by the sound of Mull. Pop. in 1841, 896.
KILNOE, a parish 33 m. SW by W of Scariff, co. Clare. Area 10,512 acres. Pop. in 1841, 3,482.
KILNSAY, a hamlet in the chapelry of Conistonwith-Kilnsay, p. of Burnsall, W. R. of Yorkshire, 11 m. E of Settle.

KILNSEA WITH SPURN, a parish in the E. R. of Yorkshire, 24 m. ESE of Hull, on a projecting point of land between the North sea and the mouth of the Humber. Area 1,130 acres. Pop. in 1831, 158; in 1841, 140. The promontory of Spurn-head or | Spurn-point, the S point of Holderness, is in this parish. On it are two lighthouses, in 53° 34′ 44" N lat., 0° 7' E long.; relative position NW 1 N, 540 ft. There is also a light vessel off Spurn-point in 53° 34' N lat., 0° 13' E long. Several towns formerly stood on this coast, which have all been long ago swallowed up by the sea. The celebrated port of Ravenspur, situated near Spurnhead, has likewise shared the same fate.
KILNWICK, a parish in the E. R. of Yorkshire, 6 m. SSW of Great Driffield. Area 3,990 acres. Pop. in 1831, 217; in 1841, 627.
KILNWICK-PERCY, a parish in the E. R. of Yorkshire, 2 m. ENE of Pocklington. Area 1,610 acres. Pop. in 1831, 49; in 1841, 58.

KILPATRICK (East), a parish partly in Dumbartonshire and partly in Stirlingshire. Area 13,440 acres. The Forth and Clyde canal traverses the parish near its S boundary, from Kelvin bridge 4 m. W. The N corner of the p. is occupied by part of the range of heights called the Kilpatrick hills or braes, which run from Strathblane W to Dumbuck, and the loftiest of which here rises about 1,200 ft. above sea-level. The parish contains the large manufacturing village of Milngavie. Of several hamlets, the only noticeable one is New Kirk, or New K., 53 m. NW of Glasgow. Pop. in 1841, 3,457.

KILPATRICK (WEST), a parish in Dumbartonshire, stretching along the Clyde, 74 m. Superficial area about 18 or 20 sq. m. One-half or rather more of the area from the N boundary downwards, is occupied by the range of the Kilpatrick hills, which terminate in Dumbuck, coming down in a rapid declivity almost to the very edge of the Clyde. The p. is remarkable for the number of its villages, and the extent and variety of its manufactures, existing in the absence of anything which can with propriety be called a town. The villages are: Old K., Duntocher, Faifley, Miltonfield, Hardgate, Yoker, Dalmuir-shore, Dalmuir, Bowling, Little-Mill, Dumbuck, and Milton; and contain, with the exception of a mere fraction, the whole of its large pop. Duntocher, Faifley, Miltonfield, and Hardgate, all situated within the range of a mile on the average, 9 m. W of Glasgow, and from \( \frac{3}{4} \) to <math>1\frac{1}{2}</math> m. W of the E boundary of the parish, contain upwards of 3,000 inhabitants. Some of the other villages are mere hamlets as to pop., yet possess importance as seats of manufacture. Pop. in 1801, 2,844; in 1831, 5,879; in 1841, 7,020.

KILPATRICK, a parish 3 m. SSW of Crosshaven, co. Cork. Pop. in 1831, 1,145; in 1841, 1,081. -Also a parish 5 m. NNE of Tipperary, co. Tipperary. Area 2,785 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,483; in 1841, 1,734.—Also a parish 3\frac{3}{4} m. SE of Castle- Pollard, co. Westmeath. Area 1,892 acres. Pop. in 1831, 416; in 1841, 447. — Also a parish 4 m. NNW of Wexford, co. Wexford. Area 2,739 acres. Pop. in 1831, 852; in 1841, 879.—Also a village in the parish of Brinny, co. Cork. Pop. in 1831, 182. -Also a parish 24 m. S by E of Castle-Carbery, co. Kildare. Area 7,076 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,227; in 1841, 1,080.
KILPEACON, a parish 43 m. S of Limerick, co. Limerick. Area of the Pobblebrien section 1,154 acres. Pop. in 1831, 578; in 1841, 424.
KILPHELAN, a parish 14 m. SSE of Mitchellstown, co. Cork. Area 524 acres. Pop. in 1841, 201.
KILPIN, a township in the p. of Howden, E. R. of Yorkshire, 2 m. SE of Howden. Pop. 393.

KILPIPE, a parish 8 m. W of Arklow, partly in co. Wexford, but chiefly in co. Wicklow. Area of the Wexford section, 3,477 acres; of the Wicklow section, 12,153 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,368; in 1841, KIL 3,400. Mount Annagh, on the S boundary, has an alt. of 1,498 ft.
KILPOOLE, a parish on the coast of co. Wicklow. Area 3,028 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,977.

KILQUANE, a parish in co. Clare, 2 m. N of Limerick. Area 3,894 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2.028; in 1841, 2,081. - Also a parish 34 m. WSW of Loughrea, co. Galway. Area 6,858 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,674; in 1841, 1,960.—Also a parish 41 m. NNW of Dingle, co. Kerry. Area 9,709 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1.674; in 1841, 1,760.
KILRAGHTIS, a parish 3 m. NNE of Ennis, co. Clare. Area 5,587 acres. Pop. in 1841, 1,995.
KILRANE, a parish 2 m. NE of Broadway, co. Wexford. Area 2,047 acres. Pop. in 1841, 720.

KILRANELAGH, a parish 3 m. E of Baltinglass, co. Wicklow. Area 4,470 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,831; in 1841, 1,800. The highest ground in the interior has an alt. of 1,252 ft.; Mount Keadeen, on the NE boundary, has an alt. of 2,143 ft.
KILRAUGHTS, a parish 4 m. E by S of Ballymoney, co. Antrim. Area 5,132 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,837; in 1841, 1,542.

KILREE, a parish 3 m. NW of Knocktopher, co. Kilkenny. Area 1,947 acres. Pop. in 1831, 652; in 1841, 720.—Also a parish partly in co. Londonderry, but chiefly in Ulster. Area of the Coleraine section, 1,061 acres; of the Loughinsholin section, 5.252 acres. Pop. in 1831, 4,262; in 1841, 4,277.— The market-town of K. is 8 m. N by W of Portglenone. Pop. in 1841, 1.191.
KILRENNY, a parish in co. Kildare, 33 m. NNW of Castle-Carbery. Area 2,617 acres. Pop. in 1831, 608; in 1841, 638.

KILRENNY, a parish and burgh in Fifeshire, upon the coast of the frith of Forth. Pop. in 1831, 1,705; in 1841, 2,039.—The burgh of K. consists of two villages: Nether-K, or Cellardykes, which is on the sea-coast, divided from Anstruther-Easter only by a small burn, and forming with it and Anstruther- Wester apparently one long town; and Upper K., situated about a m. inland. By the reform bill it was conjoined with Cupar, St. Andrews, Crail, Anstruther-Easter and Wester, and Pittenweem, in sending one member to parl. Constituency in 1840, 51; in 1848, 53. Nether-K., or Cellardykes, is a fishing-station of considerable importance.
KIL-RHEDDYN, a parish partly in Carmarthenshire, and partly in Pembrokeshire, 4 m. SW of Newcastle-Emlyn. Pop. in 1841, 1,108.
KILRICKILL, a parish 4 m. ENE of Loughrea, co. Galway. Area 5,947 acres. Pop. in 1841, 1,737,
KILROAN, a parish 3 m. S of Kinsale, co. Cork. Area 1,140 acres. Pop. in 1831, 641; in 1841, 731,

KILRONAN, a parish 8 m. N by W of Carrickon-Shannon, forming the N extremity of co. Roscommon. Area 16,356 acres, of which 1,315 acres are part of Lough Allen, and 313 acres are in Lough Meelagh. Pop. in 1831, 6,940; in 1841, 7,085. The highest ground is on the W border, and has an alt. of 1,098 ft. In addition to coal and metal mining operations in the p., considerable employment exists in the manufacture of sandstone and limestone flags for tombstones.—Also a parish in co. Waterford, 31 m. SW by S of Clonmel. Area 16,701 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,914; in 1841, 4,772.—Also a parish 3\frac{1}{2} m. SW of Waterford, co. Waterford. Area 546 acres. Pop. in 1831, 112; in 1841, 126.
KILROOT, a parish 2 m. NE by E of Carrickfergus, co. Antrim. Area 2,418 acres. Pop. in 1831, 536; in 1841, 735.
KILROSS, a parish 24 m. E by N of Coloony, co. Sligo. Area 3,932 acres. Pop. in 1841, 1,633.

KILROSSANTY, a parish 33 m. WSW of Kilmacthomas, co. Waterford. Area 17,416 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,019; in 1841, 3,615. The whole of the N district of the p., and part of the W district, are portions of the Monavoulagh mountains. The two principal summits in the N have alts. of 2,180 and 2,387 ft.; and the two principal on the W of 1,321 and 1,952 ft.; but the loftiest is the monarch-summit, Seafin, on the NW boundary.
KILRUANE, a parish 31 m. NE of Nenagh, co. Tipperary. Area of the Lower Ormond section, 1,029 acres; of the Upper Ormond section, 2,881 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,384; in 1841, 1,444.

KILRUSH, a parish and town on the S coast of co. Clare. Area of p. 15,658 acres. Pop. in 1831, 9,732; in 1841, 11.385. The bay of Poolnasherry forms part of the W boundary; and the estuary of the Shannon bounds the whole of the S.—The seaport stands at the head of the creek of K., 13 m. NNE of Inniscattery, and 27 m. SW of Ennis. The principal parts of the town are a spacious central square, and two spacious streets running parallel with each other. The market-house, in the centre of the square, is a handsome structure. The custom-house is a neat modern building. The bridewell is the largest building of its class in the co. The creek or natural harbour is dry at low water; admits only small craft even with the tide; and is provided with two small quays and a patent slip. The fishing trade of the town, as to both capture and sale, is somewhat extensive. About 20 small hookers, employing about 200 persons, belong strictly to itself; and are engaged in fishing and in dredging for oysters; and numerous fishing-craft have their rendezvous and market at K., and are employed in both the herring and the general fishery. Enormous quantities of turf are sent from K., and from the bay of Poolnasherry, to the city of Limerick, and are supplied from a tract of 24,000 acres of turbary, which commences in the immediate vicinity of the town, and extends to Moore bay and Dunbeg on the Atlantic. It is thought that the value of the turf sent off annually amounts to upwards of £10,000. The facility of conveyance by steam between Limerick and K. has very greatly increased the latter's prosperity.—The manufactures are chiefly for homeconsumption, and consist of frieze, flannel, stockings, shoes, nails, strong sheeting, and a narrow and serviceable kind of linen called bandlecloth.—Pop. in 1831, 3,996; in 1841, 5,071.—Also a parish <math>5\frac{1}{4}</math> m. S by E of Kildare, co. Kildare. Area 4,076 acres. Pop. in 1841, 577.—Also a parish 1½ m. W by S of Freshford, co. Kilkenny. Pop. in 1831, 752.—Also a parish 3 m. WNW of Dungarvan, co. Waterford. Area 1,522 acres. Pop. in 1831, 724; in 1841, 723. -Also a parish 23 m. E by S of Newtownbarry, co. Wexford. Area 11,385 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,731; in 1841, 3,158.
KILSALLAGHAN, a parish 32 m. W by N of Swords, co. Dublin. Area 2,730 acres. Pop. in 1831, 581; in 1841, 548.
KILSARAN, a parish 1 m. S of Castle-Bellingham, co. Louth. Area 3,393 acres. Pop. 2,098.
KILSCANLAN, a parish 43 m. SE of New Ross, co. Wexford. Area 1,154 acres. Pop. in 1841, 404.
KILSCANNEL, a parish 24 m. WSW of Rathkeale, co. Limerick. Area 3,203 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,031; in 1841, 1,049.
KILSCORAN, a parish 11 m. N by E of Broadway, co. Wexford. Area 2,151 acres. Pop. in 1831, 616; in 1841, 666.

KILSEILY, a parish in co. Clare. Area 11,102 acres. Pop. in 1831, 4,227; in 1841, 4,469. The summit of Knockaplunta, nearly in the centre of the p., has an alt. of 843 ft.; another summit, 12 m. to the W of it, has an alt. of 1,018 ft. KIL co. Sligo. Area 5,505 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,034; in 1841, 2,411.
KILSHANE, a parish 24 m. ESE of Tipperary. co. Tipperary. Area 1,424 acres. Pop. in 1841, 615.
KILSHANIG, a parish 3 m. SW of Mallow, co. Cork. Area 27,595 acres. Pop. in 1841, 9,348.
KILSHANNY, a parish 2 m. N of Ennistymon, eo. Clare. Area 5,805 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,114.
KILSHARVAN, a parish 3 m. S of Drogheda, co. Meath. Area of the Lower Duleek section 1.528 acres; of the Upper Duleek section 568 acres. Pop. in 1831, 487; in 1841, 440.

KILSHEELAN, a parish 43 m. W by N of Carrick-on-Suir, partly in co. Tipperary, partly in co. Waterford. Area 8,9774 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,570; in 1841, 1,936. The v. of K. stands on the l. bank of the Suir. Pop. in 1831, 290; in 1841, 435.
KILSHINANE, a parish 5 m. S by E of Listowel, co. Kerry. Area 13,478 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,271.
KILSHINE, a parish 4 m. S of Nobber, co. Meath. Area 1.543 acres. Pop. in 1841, 609.
KILSILLAGH, a parish 5 m. ESE of Clonakilty, co. Cork. Area 245 acres. Pop. in 1841, 188.
KILSKEERY, a parish in co. Tyrone, containing the town of Trillick. Area 20,438 acres. Pop. 9,352.
KILSKYRE, a parish in co. Meath, containing the town of Crossakeel. Area 11,724 acres. Pop. in 1831, 4,537; in 1841, 5,014. The hamlet of K. is 11 m. SSE of Crossakeel.

KILSPINDIE, a parish in the eastern part of Perthshire. A considerable number of the pop. are linen-weavers, in the employ of the manufacturers of Dundee. About a m. from the E angle of the p. is the v. of Rait. Pop. in 1831, 760; in 1841, 709.

KILSYTH, a parish in Stirlingshire. Area 24 sq. m., or about 15,000 acres. The S district of the p., or nearly one-half of the whole area, is the highest part of that strath which runs W from the carse of Falkirk, and is traversed by the Forth and Clyde canal. The p. is singularly rich in both the number and the utility of its minerals. Coal is very abundant, stretching in thick seams from 4 to 16 fath. beneath the surface, and probably existing in other and equally rich seams at a greater depth. Limestone, consisting of a conglomeration of small shells of all sizes, from an inch in diam. till they become invisible to the naked eye, and containing the greatest proportion of calcareous earth and the least proportion of sand of probably any limestone in Scotland, is extensively worked, and in great request. Freestone of a beautiful white colour offers an abundant supply to demands at a distance for the materials of ornamental masonry. Iron-stone has, for three-fourths of a century, been worked here by the Carron company, and occurs in a variety of forms. Balls or round masses, uniform in shape, and having the outline of a flat-topped loaf or apple-pudding, were long considered the richest form in which it occurred. A large vein of blackband iron-stone has recently been discovered. A seam of stone, from 20 to 30 ft. in thickness, has furnished an annual supply of many thousand tons for paving the streets of Glasgow. A vein of copper was wrought during last century. Pop. in 1801, 1,762; in 1831, 4,297; in 1841, 5,613. —The town of K. is <math>\frac{3}{4}</math> m. N of the Forth and Clyde canal, 5½ m. from Cumbernauld, and 12½ m. from Glasgow. Pop. in 1841, 4,106.
KILTALE, a parish 43 m. WNW of Dunshaughlin, co. Meath. Area 1,018 acres. Pop. in 1841, 419.
KILTALLA, a parish in co. Kerry, containing the town of Castlemain. Area 4,757 acres. Pop. 1,303.
KILTAMAGH, a village in the p. of Killeden, co. Mayo, 5<sup>1</sup>/<sub>4</sub> m. NE of Balla.
KILTARLITY, a large and mountainous parish

KILSHALVEE, a parish 43 m. S of Ballymote, | in Inverness-shire, formed by the union of the parishes of K. and Conveth. It is, including the mission of Strathglass, 40 m. in length, and about 8 m. in breadth. Pop. in 1831, 2,715; in 1841, 2,869.
KILTARTAN, a parish in co. Galway, containing part of the town of Gort. Area 5,725 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,930; in 1841, 2,462.
KILTEAL, a parish 23 m. NW of the town of Stradbally, Queen's co. Area of the Stradbally section 1,561 acres; of the Maryborough section 1,991 acres. Pop. of the whole in 1841, 1,067.
KILTEARN, a parish in Ross-shire, on the N side of the frith of Cromarty. Benwyvis is in this p. Pop. in 1831, 1,605; in 1841, 1,436.

KILTEEL, or EADESTOWN, a parish and village of co. Kildare, 54 m. E by N of Naas. Area 3,435 acres. Pop. in 1831, 755; in 1841, 797. The E district contains Cupidstown hill. Alt. above sea-level 1,248 ft. The v. of K. stands on the road from Ballymore-Eustace to Rathcoole, 61 m. NNE of Ballymore-Eustace. Pop. in 1841, 168.
KILTEELY, or LISTEELY, a parish 2 m. SW by S of Pallas-Green, co. Limerick. Area of the Coonagh section 1,655 acres; of the Small County section 1,529 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,049.
KILTEEVAN, a parish 3 m. E of Roscommon, co. Roscommon. Area 8,411 acres, of which 959 acres are in Lough Ree. Pop. in 1841, 2,818.
KILTEEVOCK, a parish 51 m. W by N of Stranorlar, co. Donegal. Area 41,131 acres. Pop. 4,864.

KILTEGAN, a parish 3 m. NW of Hacketstown, partly in co. Carlow, but chiefly in co. Wicklow. Area of the Rathvilly section 815 acres; of the Talbotstown section 10,931 acres; of the Ballinacor section 4,203 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,823; in 1841, 3,969. Ballinabarny Gap, on the E road from Donard to Hacketstown, has an alt. of 1,178 ft.; Slieveragh, on the N boundary, has an alt. of 1,560 ft.; and Carrig, in the interior, has an alt. of 1,845 ft.—Also a parish 13 m. NW of Clonmel, co. Tipperary. Area 1,069 acres. Pop. in 1831, 190; in 1841, 283.

KILTENNEL, a parish 3½ m. E by N of Borris, co. Carlow. Area 11,170 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,206; in 1841, 3,544. Mount Leinster, near the NE extremity, has an alt. of 2,610 ft.; Knockroe, on the E border, 1,746 ft.; Tomduff, on the N boundary, 1,308 ft.; the mountain of the Nine Stones, on the N boundary, 1,726 ft.—Also a parish on the coast of co. Wexford, 24 m. E of Gorey. Area 4,125 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,137; in 1841, 1,279.

KILTERNAN, a parish 3½ m. SSE of Dundrum, co. Dublin. Area 3,165 acres. Pop. in 1831, 913; in 1841, 1,019. A mountain on the W border has an alt. of 1,618 ft.; and Prince William's Seat, on the S boundary, has an alt. of 1,825 ft.
KILTESKILL, a parish in co. Galway, 34 m. SE of Loughrea. Area of the Loughrea section 1,716 acres; of the Leitrim section 2,979 acres. Pop. 802.
KILTESKIN, a parish 21 m. SSW of Cloyne, co. Cork. Area 1,145 acres. Pop. in 1841, 703.
KILTEYNAN, a parish 2½ m. SE of Fethard, co. Tipperary. Area 5,102 acres. Pop. in 1841, 1,296.
KILTHOMAS, a parish 33 m. NE of Gort, co. Galway. Area of the Loughrea section 7,125 acres; of the Kiltartan section 4,584 acres. Pop. 3,278.

KILTOGHART, a parish in co. Leitrim, containing the villages of K., Leitrim, Drumshambo, and Jamestown, and the greater part of the town of Carrick-on-Shannon. Area 30,494 acres, of which 1,717 acres are in Lough Allen. Pop. in 1831, 16,434; in 1841, 17,581. The N district is mountainous and waste, and attains in the summit of Slieve-an-Erin an extreme alt. of 1,922 ft.

KILTON, a parish in Somersetshire, 101 m. NW by W of Bridgewater, on the Bristol channel. Area 1,560 acres. Pop. in 1831, 141; in 1841, 161.—Also KIL a township in the p. of Brotton, N. R. of Yorkshire, 6 m. NE of Guisborough. Pop. 86.
KILTONANLEA, or Doonass, a parish in co. Clare, 3\frac{1}{4} m. S by W of O'Brien's Bridge, containing the v. of Cloonlara. Area 7,627 acres. Pop. 4,016.
KILTOOM, a parish 44 m. NNW of Athlone, co. Roscommon. Area 13,246 acres, of which 2,757 acres are in Lough Ree. Pop. in 1841, 4,150.
KILTOOMY, a parish 81 m. SW of Listowel, co. Kerry. Area 5,866 acres. Pop. in 1841, 2,043.
KILTORA, a parish 42 m. ESE of Tubbercurry, partly in co. Mayo, partly in co. Sligo. Area of the Mayo section 3,645 acres; of the Sligo section 3,238 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,481; in 1841, 2,613.
KILTORAGHT, a parish 23 m. SE of Kilfenora, co. Clare. Area 3,091 acres. Pop. in 1841, 1,074.
KILTORMER, a parish and village 51 m. WNW of Eyrecourt, co. Galway. Area 6,898 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,133; in 1841, 2,089.
KILTRELLIG, a village in the p. of Kilballyhone, co. Clare, at the head of Kilbaha bay, 3 m. E by N of Loop-head. Pop. 304.
KILTRISK, a parish 5 m. NE of Oulart, co. Wexford. Area 3,684 acres. Pop. in 1841, 757.
KILTRUSTAN, a parish in co. Roseommon, containing on its S border a part of the town of Strokestown. Area 6,339 acres. Pop. in 1841, 3,938.

KILTUBRID, a parish 3 m. ESE of Drumshambo, co. Leitrim, containing the v. of Keshcarrigan. Area 15,608 acres. Pop. in 1831, 6,508; in 1841, 7,228. Slieve-an-Erin, on the NW border, has an alt. of 1,922 ft.

KILTULLAGH, a parish 43 m. E by S of Athenry, co. Galway. Area of the Kilconnel section 1,171 acres; of the Athenry section 7,776 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,069; in 1841, 3,244.—Also a parish 54 m. WSW of the town of Castlerea, co. Roscommon. Area 24,713 acres, of which 373 acres are in Lough O'Flyn. Pop. in 1831, 7,110; in 1841, 7,664.
KILTURK, a parish 54 m. WSW of Broadway, co. Wexford. Area 2,206 acres. Pop. in 1841, 912.
KILTYCLOGHER, a village in the p. of Cloonclare, co. Leitrim, 2½ m. NW of the head of Upper Lough Macnean. Pop. in 1841, 244.
KILVARNET, a parish 34 m. NW of Ballymote, co. Sligo. Area 6,696 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,360; in 1841, 2,352. The principal hamlet is Ballinacarrow.
KILVE, a parish in Somersetshire, 113 m. NW by W of Bridgewater. Area 1,680 acres. Pop. 240.
KILVEMNON, a parish 7 m. ENE of Fethard, co. Tipperary. Area 10,551 acres. Pop. in 1831, 4,530; in 1841, 4,983. The highest ground on the S boundary has an alt. of 1,088 ft. above sea-level.
KILVERSTONE, a parish in Norfolk, 13 m. ENE of Thetford, in the vale of the Thet. Area 2,540 acres. Pop. in 1831, 36; in 1841, 47.
KILVINE, a parish and village 62 m. ESE of Claremorris, co. Mayo. Area 5,426 acres. Pop. 2,236.
KILVINGTON, a parish in Nottinghamshire, 7 m. S of Newark-upon-Trent. Area 900 acres. Pop. 56.
KILVINGTON (South), a parish in the N. R. of Yorkshire, 1 m. NNW of Thirsk. The township of South K. is included within the boundaries of the parl. borough of Thirsk. Area 2,610 acres. Pop. 402.
KILVOLANE, a parish in co. Tipperary, containing the town of Newport-Tip. Area 8,678 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,656; in 1841, 4,254.
KILWATERMOY, a parish 2 m. NE of Tallow, co. Waterford. Area 6,556 acres. Pop. 2,400.

KILWAUGHTER, a parish 1 m. WSW of Larne, co. Antrim. Area 9,803 acres. Pop. in 1831, 2,016; in 1841, 2,164. Agnew's hill, in the W, a long range of precipitous basalt, has an extreme alt. of 1,556 ft. above sea-level.
KILWEILAGH, a parish 2 m. SW of Castletowndelvin, co. Westmeath. Area 8,145 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,315; in 1841, 1,229.
KILWHELAN, a parish 13 m. S by E of Mitchelstown, co. Cork. Area 524 acres. Pop. 201.

KILWINNING, a parish and town in the district of Cunningham, Ayrshire. Area of p. 171 sq. m. Garnock and Lugton waters intersect the p., the former SE and the latter SW, making a confluence about a mile below Eglinton-castle. Pop. in 1801, 2,700; in 1831, 3,772; in 1841, 5,251.—The town of K. is pleasantly situated on a gentle rising ground on the r. bank of the Garnock, 2 m. from the nearest part of the frith of Clyde, and 23 m. NW of Irvine. It consists principally of one street, winged by some lanes, and of some rows of modern houses. With the exception of a few families, the whole pop., not only of the town, but of the landward part of the p., are of the working-classes, principally hand-loom weavers, shopkeepers, labourers, and colliers.

KILWORTH, a parish and town in co. Cork. Area of p. 5,458 acres. Pop. in 1831, 3,038; in 1841, 3,246.—The town of K. stands on the Dublin and Cork mail-road, near the I. bank of the Funcheon, and at the base of the Kilworth mountains, 24 m. NNE of Fermoy. It consists principally of one long street, and has some good houses. Pop. 1,772.
KILWORTH, a village in the township of Delaware, in Upper Canada, on the river Thames, 5 m. from Delaware. Pop. 160.
KILWORTH (NORTH), or KILWORTH-ABBAS, a parish in Leicestershire, 5 m. E by S of Lutterworth. Area 2,230 acres. Pop. in 1831, 390; in 1841, 422.
KILWORTH (South), a parish in Leicestershire, 45 m. ESE of Lutterworth, on the river Avon. Area 1,470 acres. Pop. in 1831, 437; in 1841, 478.
KIL-Y-BEBILL, or CIL-Y-BEBILL (LOWER and UPPER), a parish in Glamorganshire, 5 m. NNW of Neath, on the river Tawe. Pop. in 1841, 731.
KIL-Y-MAENLLWYD, or CIL-Y-MAENLLWYD, a parish partly in the co. of Pembroke, but chiefly in the co. of Carmarthen, 61 m. NNE of Narberth. Pop. in 1831, 607; in 1841, 520.

KIM, or KEEM, a river of Hindostan, in the prov. of Gujerat, and district of Baroch. It has its source in the mountains about 20 m. S of Naundode; runs WSW; and after a course of about 75 m. throws itself into the gulf of Cambaye, a little to the S of the mouth of the Nerbudda. Its embouchure is large, but is much impeded by sand-banks.
KIMADO, a town of the island of Celebes, on the W side of Tolo bay.

KIMANIS, a port of Borneo, in Borneo Proper, in N lat. 5° 8', on a small river, 10 m. from its mouth, at the foot of a beautiful range of hills inhabited by Orang-Idan. It was ceded to the English by the sultan of Sulo, with the country extending from this point to Cape Kaniongan on the straits of Macassar. Cinnamon and cassia are abundantly produced in the vicinity.

KIMBERLEY, a parish and village of Norfolk, 3 m. NW of Wymondham, near the source of the Yare. Area 1,460 acres. Pop. in 1831, 138; in 1841, 148.—Also a hamlet in the p. of Greasley, Nottinghamshire, 6 m. NW by W of Nottingham. Pop. in 1831, 1,700; in 1841, 1,778.
KIMBERTON, a village of East Pikeland township, Chester co., in the state of Pennsylvania, U. S., 75 m. ESE of Harrisburg.
KIMBERWORTH, a township in the p. and 13 m. W by N of Rotherham, W. R. of Yorkshire, in the line of the Sheffield and Rotherham railway. Area 2,940 acres. Pop. in 1831, 4,031; in 1841, 5,066.
KIMBLE, a township of Athens co., in the state of Ohio, U. S. Pop. in 1840, 762. KIN
KIMBLE (GREAT and LITTLE), two parishes in Buckinghamshire, distant respectively from Wendover 31 and 3 m. WSW. Area of former, 2,570 acres. Pop. 489. Area of latter, 750 acres. Pop. 177.

KIMBOLTON, a parish of Herefordshire, 23 m. NE of Leominster. Area 4,640 acres. Pop. in 1831, 719; in 1841, 715.—Also a parish and market-town 10 m. W by S of Huntingdon, and 63 m. NNW of London, on the Kym. Area 6,200 acres. Pop. in 1831, 1,584; in 1841, 1,634.
KIMCOTE, or KILNCOTE, a parish in Leicestershire, 31 m. NE by E of Lutterworth. Area 1,710 acres. Pop. in 1831, 490; in 1841, 552.
KIMEDY, a town of Hindostan, in the presidency of Madras, and prov. of the Northern Circars, dist. and 84 m. SSW of Gangam.
KIMERIDGE, or KOMMERIDGE, a parish and village of Dorsetshire, in the island of Purbeck, 31 m. SW by W of Corfe-Castle. Area 1,570 acres. Pop. in 1831, 124; in 1841, 154.
KIMELISZKI, a town of Russia in Europe, in the gov. and 29 m. ENE of Vilna, district and 27 m. SSW of Swieciany.

KIMITO, an island of Russia in Europe, in the Baltic, off the W coast of Finland, in the gov. of Abo, in N lat. 60° 24', E long. 22° 10'. It is 12 m. in length from N to S, and 9 m. in medium breadth. It is granitic, with superstrata of limestone, and contains some iron. It contains several villages, one of the same name; and, inclusive of the adjacent islets, possesses about 6,500 inhabitants.
KIMMAGE, a hamlet in co. Dublin, 2 m. SW of Dublin castle.
KIMMAR'S PORT, a creek in Ardglass harbour, co. Down.
KIMMOLI. See ARGENTIERA.
KIMO, a town of Senegambia, in the kingdom of Kasson, 35 m. SE of Kuniakary, on an affluent of the Senegal.

KIMPINA, a town of Turkey in Europe, in Lower Wallachia, in the district of Prahova, 23 m. NE of Tergovist, and 51 m. N of Bucharest, on the r. bank of the Rahova. It has several churches, a convent, and a custom-house; and is the entrepot for all the goods from Cronstadt. In the vicinity are extensive salt-mines, which furnish annually 150,000 quintals of that article. Bitumen is also found in large quantities in the locality.

KIMPOLUNG, a town of Turkey in Europe, in Upper Wallachia, capital of the district of Muscel, 36 m. ENE of Rimnik, in a fine valley, 6 m. S of the pass of Tarzburg. Pop. 4,000, chiefly Greeks. It covers a large area, and contains several Greek churches, a Catholic church and convent, two Greek convents, and a district school. It possesses an active trade, and has a large annual fair. It was formerly a place of great manufacturing importance. In the vicinity are the ruins of the Tselatia niagra or Black fortress.
KIMPOLUNG (Moldauisch), a town of Austria in Gallicia, in the circle and 60 m. SSW of Tschernowitz, and 32 m. ENE of Suczawa, on the r. bank of the Moldawa. Pop. 2,623.
